7:00:47 Literally gets me emotional. I'm so happy for Rekkles. He might not have played a game at Worlds but his story should truly be eye opening and motivating for everyone. His story is about questioning if you've given all you can, if you have new peaks to reach, and what you're willing to risk to accomplish it: He gave up being EU's best ADC to take a chance on himself. He literally said that he didn't believe he could win a Worlds title on a western team or as an ADC. So he decided to become one of very few western players to move to an Asian region. He decided to role swap which very few players have found success attempting. He battled his way through tryouts as a SUPPPORT for T1, the greatest Org in eSports, after role swapping and he got the position on the same Challenger team that brought us the T1 GOATs. He moved countries, learned a new language, learned a new culture, all because he believed it was the only way to win Worlds. And there he is. Hoisting the Worlds trophy. Congratulations Rekkles. You earned that as much as anyone else on that stage.

Yes he's one of, if not the best mid laner in the world not named Faker, and yes it's his signature champion, but I have been completely overwhelmed by how underwhelming Ahri has been all worlds. And despite getting first blood on Ahri, Knight's performance didn't change that, not because he played badly but because I think the champion just isn't good enough to play in pro in her current state.
